SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Apr-06-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Small Claims Short Cause Trial
Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk Rene Pascual. Not reported (501)

Appearances:
Plaintiff: Arthur Brunwasser- in pro per
Defendant: Ragui H. Michael-in pro per

This matter comes on calendar in this dept. for hearing on the merits of the plaintiff's claim, re;non-payment of legal his legal services in the sum of $3,651. with interest. After both parties were duly sworn, parties individual statements commence;

Plaintiff in pro per Mr. Brunwasser presents the ff. documents to the Court; Accounting inventory of his services, from Nov. 3, 2003 to present, Notice of Right to Arbitrate, Notice of Stay & Declaration of SF Bar Asso. lifting the stay.
Defendant in pro per Mr. Michael; maintains that there was no proper service; ff. documents submitted for consideration; Copy of the e-mail terminating plaintiff's services, copy of the Plaintiff's Motion to be relieved as counsel-granted and copy of defendant's declaration re; his defense for non-payment. Mr.Michael claims that he already paid the plaintiff almost $5,000.
Ruling; Argued & the court ruled that there was no proof of proper service. The matter is taken under submission. Court to issue order.

APPEARANCES:

Defendant Ragui H. Michael is present In Pro Per.

This matter comes on calendar for a hearing regarding service of the notice of appeal on the defendant. The court decides to continue this matter to June 6, 2008 at 9:30 a.m. Litigants, if needed, are to submit briefing to the court regarding the issue of service. Any paperwork submitted should be properly served on the other side, filed with the court and have a proof of service as well as provide a courtesy copy to Department 611.
